title: Steganthera dentata
taxonomy: Steganthera dentata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Steganthera, which comprises several species of flowering plants. The Annonaceae family, to which Steganthera dentata belongs, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard-apple family. Steganthera dentata is native to the tropical regions of the world and is often found in humid and subtropical environments. The exact classification of Steganthera dentata is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaf shape, flower structure, and fruit type.

intro: Steganthera dentata is a plant species belonging to the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the "toothed Steganthera" due to its distinctive tooth-like projections on the edges of its leaves. Traditionally, Steganthera dentata has been used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Steganthera dentata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Steganthera dentata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of tinctures and capsules to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits.
